rings.name: ADOPT
rings.description: Proven, production-ready technologies that Red Hat and IBM actively recommend and ship.

rings.name: TRIAL
rings.description: Technologies gaining traction and recommended for broader adoption with appropriate evaluation.

rings.name: ASSESS
rings.description: Emerging technologies worth exploring, but not yet ready for production at scale.

rings.name: HOLD
rings.description: Products that are officially end-of-life, discontinued, or sold off — avoid for new projects and plan migration.

entries.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL)
entries.description: The world's leading enterprise Linux platform. Foundation for Red Hat's entire portfolio and the primary OS for hybrid cloud workloads.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform 4
entries.description: Enterprise Kubernetes platform with built-in operations, security, and developer tooling. The cornerstone of Red Hat's hybrid cloud strategy.

timeline.description: OCP 4.0 GA — new Operator-based architecture, rough edges expected
OCP 4.4 — stable enough for broad production adoption

entries.title: KVM Hypervisor
entries.description: Kernel-based Virtual Machine — the Linux hypervisor at the core of Red Hat's virtualisation and OpenShift Virtualization workloads.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Service on AWS (ROSA)
entries.description: Fully-managed OpenShift service on AWS, jointly supported by Red Hat and AWS. Enables cloud-native development without cluster administration.

entries.title: IBM Cloud
entries.description: IBM's public cloud platform spanning 60+ data centres with strong AI, security, and hybrid cloud capabilities.

timeline.description: IBM Bluemix public beta launched
Bluemix GA — viable public cloud option
Rebranded IBM Cloud; expanded regions and enterprise services

entries.title: IBM Z (Mainframe)
entries.description: IBM's mainframe platform (z16, z17) with on-chip AI via Telum processors. Handles billions of transactions daily with industry-leading reliability.

entries.title: IBM Power Systems
entries.description: High-performance RISC servers (Power10, Power11) for AI inference, ERP, and cloud workloads. Primary platform for AIX and IBM i.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Virtualization
entries.description: Run and manage virtual machines alongside containers on OpenShift, powered by KubeVirt. The strategic replacement for Red Hat Virtualization.

timeline.description: Container Native Virtualization (CNV) tech preview
OpenShift Virtualization 2.4 GA — first fully supported release

entries.title: MicroShift
entries.description: Lightweight OpenShift optimised for resource-constrained edge devices. Provides Kubernetes API compatibility with a tiny footprint.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift 3.x
entries.description: First-generation OpenShift based on Docker and Ansible. Extended Life Support ended June 2024 — all users should have migrated to OCP 4.

timeline.description: ELS ended June 2024

entries.title: OpenShift Online
entries.description: Red Hat's hosted OpenShift SaaS. Shut down September 2023; users migrated to ROSA or Developer Sandbox.

timeline.description: Service shut down

entries.title: Red Hat Virtualization (RHV)
entries.description: oVirt-based enterprise VM platform. EOL August 2026; Red Hat recommends migrating to OpenShift Virtualization.

timeline.description: RHV / oVirt widely adopted for enterprise VM workloads
EOL announced; OpenShift Virtualization named strategic replacement
EOL August 2026 — begin migration planning now

entries.title: Red Hat build of Quarkus
entries.description: Cloud-native Java framework optimised for Kubernetes. Supersonic startup times and low memory via GraalVM native compilation or JVM mode.

timeline.description: Quarkus 0.x public preview — Supersonic Subatomic Java announced
Quarkus 1.0 GA — production-ready for early adopters
Broad enterprise adoption; native image support mature

entries.title: Red Hat JBoss EAP
entries.description: Enterprise Java application server (Jakarta EE / MicroProfile). EAP 8 targets Java 17/21 and modern cloud deployments.

timeline.description: JBoss 3/4 — open-source J2EE gaining enterprise traction
JBoss EAP 5 — fully supported enterprise Java standard

entries.title: Open Liberty
entries.description: IBM's open-source Jakarta EE and MicroProfile runtime. Lightweight, composable feature set ideal for cloud-native Java microservices.

timeline.description: Open Liberty 1.0 open-sourced from WebSphere Liberty
Mature, cloud-native Java runtime; preferred for new IBM workloads

entries.title: Eclipse OpenJ9 / IBM Semeru Runtimes
entries.description: High-performance JVM with significantly lower memory footprint and fast startup. Packaged as IBM Semeru Runtimes (certified OpenJDK distributions).

entries.title: MicroProfile
entries.description: Community specification for cloud-native Java microservices. Supported by Red Hat (Quarkus) and IBM (Open Liberty, WebSphere Liberty).

entries.title: Red Hat build of Camel K
entries.description: Serverless integration runtime for running Apache Camel routes natively on Kubernetes with minimal configuration.

timeline.description: Camel K 1.0 — experimental Kubernetes-native Camel
Included in Red Hat Integration; gaining traction for serverless integration

entries.title: GraalVM Native Image
entries.description: Ahead-of-time compiler turning JVM applications into fast-starting native binaries. Mandrel (Red Hat's distribution) targets Quarkus use cases.

entries.title: JBoss EAP 5 / 6
entries.description: Legacy JBoss application server versions based on older Java EE specifications. Long past EOL — migrate to JBoss EAP 8 or Quarkus.

timeline.description: JBoss EAP 4/5 — de facto enterprise Java standard
EAP 7 released; EAP 5/6 users urged to upgrade
EOL — migrate to JBoss EAP 8 or Quarkus

entries.title: Red Hat Mobile Application Platform
entries.description: Mobile backend-as-a-service and app development platform. Officially discontinued; capabilities absorbed into broader OpenShift tooling.

timeline.description: Discontinued

entries.title: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform
entries.description: Enterprise IT automation platform for configuration management, provisioning, and orchestration. Agentless, YAML-based, and widely adopted across hybrid cloud.

timeline.description: Ansible open-source project founded by Michael DeHaan
Red Hat acquires Ansible — enterprise product launched
Ansible Automation Platform 1.0 — enterprise standard for hybrid cloud automation

entries.title: Podman
entries.description: Daemonless, rootless container engine with a Docker-compatible CLI. Default container tool in RHEL and Fedora.

timeline.description: Podman 0.x — daemonless container tool, early adopter phase
Included in RHEL 8 as the default container CLI
RHEL 9 ships Podman 4; Docker CLI compatibility mature

entries.title: Helm
entries.description: The Kubernetes package manager. First-class support in OpenShift via the OperatorHub and Developer Catalog.

entries.title: Operator Framework / OLM
entries.description: SDK and lifecycle manager for building and running Kubernetes Operators. Originated at Red Hat and donated to CNCF.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Pipelines
entries.description: Tekton-based cloud-native CI/CD for Kubernetes. Pipelines run as isolated containers with no shared state.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift GitOps
entries.description: Argo CD-based GitOps operator for OpenShift. Ensures cluster state matches what is declared in Git.

timeline.description: Argo CD gaining traction; GitOps pattern emerging in OpenShift
OpenShift GitOps 1.0 GA — Argo CD as supported product
Recommended by default for application delivery on OpenShift

entries.title: Red Hat build of Keycloak
entries.description: Enterprise identity and access management. Supports OIDC, SAML, and social login. The current Red Hat IAM product (formerly Red Hat SSO).

timeline.description: Red Hat SSO 6 (based on Keycloak) launched for JBoss middleware
Red Hat SSO 7 — standard enterprise IAM across the portfolio
Rebranded 'Red Hat build of Keycloak' — same product, modernised packaging

entries.title: Red Hat Advanced Cluster Security (ACS)
entries.description: Kubernetes-native security platform (StackRox acquisition). Provides vulnerability management, policy enforcement, and runtime threat detection.

timeline.description: StackRox standalone Kubernetes security platform — evaluate for cloud-native security
Red Hat acquires StackRox — integration into OpenShift begins
ACS 3.70 fully integrated with OpenShift; recommended for all OCP deployments

entries.title: IBM Turbonomic
entries.description: AI-powered application resource management. Continuously optimises performance and cost across hybrid cloud by making autonomous workload placement decisions.

entries.title: IBM Instana Observability
entries.description: Automated APM and observability with sub-second granularity and AI-driven root-cause analysis for containerised and microservice architectures.

entries.title: Red Hat Developer Hub
entries.description: Enterprise internal developer portal built on Backstage. Provides software catalogs, TechDocs, golden-path templates, and plugin ecosystem.

timeline.description: Red Hat Developer Hub Tech Preview — Backstage-based portal in evaluation
RHDH 1.0 GA — generally available for enterprise deployment

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Dev Spaces
entries.description: Browser-based cloud development environments running on OpenShift. Successor to CodeReady Workspaces; based on Eclipse Che.

entries.title: Ansible Lightspeed
entries.description: AI-powered Ansible content assistant using IBM watsonx. Generates playbook tasks from natural language prompts inside VS Code.

timeline.description: Announced at Red Hat Summit 2023 — Technical Preview with IBM watsonx
GA — first AI assistant in Ansible Automation Platform

entries.title: IBM watsonx Code Assistant
entries.description: AI code generation and explanation powered by Granite models. Supports Java modernisation, Ansible, and Z/COBOL use cases.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Lightspeed
entries.description: Generative AI assistant embedded in the OpenShift web console. Answers operational questions and helps with troubleshooting in natural language.

entries.title: InstructLab
entries.description: Open-source tooling for aligning and fine-tuning LLMs using a taxonomy-driven data synthesis approach. Co-developed by Red Hat and IBM.

entries.title: Minishift
entries.description: Local OpenShift 3 environment. Unmaintained since the OpenShift 4 launch in 2019 — replaced by Red Hat OpenShift Local (CRC).

timeline.description: Unmaintained; replaced by OpenShift Local

entries.title: Red Hat Container Development Kit (CDK)
entries.description: Pre-configured development environment for OpenShift 3. Superseded by Red Hat OpenShift Local (formerly CodeReady Containers). No longer available.

timeline.description: Replaced by OpenShift Local

entries.title: CodeReady Workspaces
entries.description: Browser-based IDE on OpenShift. Officially retired and replaced by Red Hat OpenShift Dev Spaces.

timeline.description: Replaced by OpenShift Dev Spaces

entries.title: Red Hat CloudForms
entries.description: Hybrid cloud management platform. Reached end of life March 2023; capabilities moved into Ansible Automation Platform and ACM.

timeline.description: CloudForms 1.0 GA — hybrid cloud management platform
Widely deployed for managing VMware, OpenStack, and OpenShift environments
Capabilities moving to Ansible and ACM; investment declining
EOL March 2023

entries.title: Red Hat Single Sign-On (SSO)
entries.description: Red Hat's Keycloak-based IAM product under the old branding. Fully replaced by the 'Red Hat build of Keycloak' from version 22 onwards.

timeline.description: Red Hat SSO 6 launched
Red Hat SSO 7 — enterprise IAM standard
Replaced by 'Red Hat build of Keycloak' — migrate to new product name and packaging

entries.title: Red Hat AMQ Streams (Apache Kafka)
entries.description: Enterprise Apache Kafka distribution for OpenShift and RHEL. Strimzi-based Operator provides lifecycle management, rebalancing, and mirror maker.

timeline.description: AMQ Streams 1.0 — Strimzi-based Kafka on OpenShift, first release
Kafka on Kubernetes mature; standard for event streaming in Red Hat portfolio

entries.title: Red Hat build of Apache Camel
entries.description: Integration framework with 300+ connectors implementing enterprise integration patterns. The strategic replacement for Red Hat Fuse.

timeline.description: Apache Camel gaining enterprise traction through Red Hat Fuse
Camel 2.x — backbone of Red Hat Fuse integration platform
Camel 3 released — Quarkus-native mode, cloud-native first
Rebranded as 'Red Hat build of Apache Camel' replacing Fuse

entries.title: IBM MQ
entries.description: Enterprise message queue for guaranteed, once-and-only-once delivery. Industry standard for financial services and transactional integration.

entries.title: IBM App Connect Enterprise
entries.description: Integration platform with AI-powered mapping, low-code flows, and 200+ pre-built connectors. Formerly IBM Integration Bus.

entries.title: IBM Db2
entries.description: IBM's flagship relational database for OLTP and analytics. Decades of enterprise adoption with deep Z and Power integration.

entries.title: IBM watsonx.ai
entries.description: AI studio for training, validating, tuning, and deploying foundation models. Hosts IBM Granite and supports third-party models.

timeline.description: Announced at Think 2023 — IBM's new AI and data platform
watsonx.ai GA — enterprise AI studio with Granite models
Rapid enterprise adoption; integrated across IBM Cloud Pak for Data

entries.title: IBM watsonx.data
entries.description: Open data lakehouse built on Presto and Apache Iceberg. Designed for cost-effective AI data preparation and analytics across hybrid cloud.

entries.title: IBM watsonx.governance
entries.description: AI governance, risk, and compliance platform. Monitors models for drift, bias, and regulatory compliance.

entries.title: IBM watsonx Assistant
entries.description: Enterprise conversational AI platform with RAG, GenAI answer generation, and multi-channel deployment capabilities.

timeline.description: Watson Assistant GA — conversational AI for enterprise chatbots
Widely deployed in banking, retail, and healthcare for virtual agents
Rebranded as IBM watsonx Assistant; RAG and GenAI capabilities added

entries.title: IBM QRadar SIEM
entries.description: Security information and event management platform for threat detection, correlation, and compliance reporting.

entries.title: IBM Guardium Data Security
entries.description: Data discovery, classification, activity monitoring, and compliance automation for structured and unstructured data.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift AI
entries.description: Data science and MLOps platform on OpenShift. Provides managed Jupyter notebooks, model serving, pipelines, and GPU resource management.

timeline.description: Open Data Hub (ODH) — upstream community project for ML on OpenShift
Red Hat OpenShift Data Science (RHODS) GA — supported MLOps platform
Rebranded Red Hat OpenShift AI; GPU scheduling and model serving added

entries.title: IBM Granite Models
entries.description: Family of open-source foundation models (code, language, time series) developed by IBM Research. Available on watsonx.ai and Hugging Face.

timeline.description: IBM Granite models unveiled at Think 2023 — enterprise-focused foundation models
Granite code and language models released on watsonx.ai and Hugging Face

entries.title: IBM Quantum
entries.description: IBM's gate-based quantum computers and cloud access platform (Qiskit Runtime). Roadmap targets fault-tolerant quantum advantage.

entries.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux AI
entries.description: Foundation model image built on RHEL for running IBM Granite models locally with hardware acceleration on bare metal or VMs.

entries.title: Red Hat AI Inference Server
entries.description: vLLM-based inference server optimised for running large language models at scale across hybrid cloud and on-premises deployments.

entries.title: Red Hat Gluster Storage
entries.description: Scale-out NAS based on GlusterFS. Reached end of life December 2024 — migrate to Red Hat Ceph / OpenShift Data Foundation.

timeline.description: Red Hat acquires Gluster Inc. — scale-out NAS for cloud storage
Widely deployed for OpenShift persistent storage and HPC
Red Hat Ceph / OpenShift Data Foundation named preferred storage path
EOL December 2024

entries.title: Red Hat Fuse 7
entries.description: Camel-based integration platform. Reached end of life June 2024 — migrate to Red Hat build of Apache Camel or Camel K.

timeline.description: Red Hat Fuse 6 — enterprise ESB and integration platform
Fuse 7 GA — widely deployed integration platform
Red Hat build of Apache Camel named strategic replacement for Fuse
EOL June 2024 — migrate to Red Hat build of Apache Camel

entries.title: Red Hat JBoss A-MQ 6
entries.description: ActiveMQ 5-based message broker. Reached end of life February 2025 — migrate to Red Hat AMQ Broker 7 (ActiveMQ Artemis).

timeline.description: EOL February 2025

entries.title: IBM Blockchain Platform (SaaS)
entries.description: IBM's managed Hyperledger Fabric SaaS on IBM Cloud. The cloud service has been wound down — the open-source Hyperledger Fabric project continues.

timeline.description: IBM Blockchain Platform launched on Bluemix — early Hyperledger Fabric SaaS
Broad availability; IBM Food Trust and TradeLens built on platform
SaaS offering wound down — Hyperledger Fabric open-source project continues

entries.title: Watson Discovery
entries.description: Watson-branded AI search and text analytics service. Actively superseded by watsonx.ai RAG capabilities; IBM is directing customers to migrate.

timeline.description: Watson Discovery launched — AI-powered search and insights
Widely adopted for document intelligence and enterprise search
Watson Discovery Premium — enterprise RAG before RAG was mainstream
Superseded by watsonx.ai RAG capabilities — IBM directing customers to migrate

entries.title: Lotus Notes / Domino
entries.description: Collaborative messaging and application platform. Sold to HCL Technologies in 2019; IBM support fully ended 2024.

timeline.description: Lotus Notes / Domino 6/7 — dominant enterprise collaboration platform
Microsoft Exchange and SharePoint eroding market share; declining investment
IBM divests to HCL Technologies — no longer an IBM product
